description_long: This is a heavy-duty sledgehammer with a robust, cylindrical metal head and a thick, straight handle. The head is solid and shows signs of extensive use, with visible dents, scratches, and some surface rust, especially on the striking faces. The handle appears to be made of wood, showing a dark, weathered finish and some chipping or wear at the end. The connection between the head and handle is tight, with the head slightly wider at the ends and more rounded on the sides. The overall appearance suggests this tool has been used for demanding tasks such as breaking concrete or driving stakes. Its utilitarian design and visible wear make it suitable for urban construction or demolition scenarios.
description: A well-worn sledgehammer with a solid metal head and a thick, weathered wooden handle, showing signs of heavy use and surface rust.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the hammer, highlighting the cylindrical metal head and the dark, worn wooden handle.
description_view_1: End-on view of the hammer, focusing on the circular striking face and the alignment of the handle with the head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, displaying the other face of the head and the handle's chipped end.
description_view_3: View from the top of the head, showing the oval shape and the attachment point of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: straight wooden handle, dark and weathered, with visible chipping and a rough texture
attribute_head_type: solid sledgehammer head, cylindrical with two flat striking faces, made of steel and showing rounded edges
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: visible dents, scratches, surface rust on the head, and chipping at the end of the handle
